# Svelte JSON View Lite — Complete Reference

> Fast, tiny JSON tree viewer for Svelte 5 — port of react-json-view-lite (MIT © 2024 AnyRoad) with runes, SSR, per-type Snippet overrides, CSS-variable theming, and zero runtime dependencies.

## Package Information

- Package: `@humanspeak/svelte-json-view-lite`
- Version: 0.1.x
- License: MIT (Copyright © 2024-2026 Humanspeak, Inc.; upstream © 2024 AnyRoad)
- Requires: Svelte 5
- Runtime dependencies: none
- Maintained by: Humanspeak, Inc.
- Homepage: https://jsonview.svelte.page
- Repository: https://github.com/humanspeak/svelte-json-view-lite
- Upstream: https://github.com/AnyRoad/react-json-view-lite

## Installation

```bash
npm install @humanspeak/svelte-json-view-lite
# or
pnpm add @humanspeak/svelte-json-view-lite
# or
yarn add @humanspeak/svelte-json-view-lite
```

## JsonView Component Props

| Prop                   | Type                                                      | Default         | Description                                                                        |
| ---------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- | --------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `data`                 | `object \| unknown[]`                                     | —               | The JSON-shaped value to render                                                    |
| `style`                | `Partial<StyleProps>`                                     | `defaultStyles` | Classname-map that themes every slot                                               |
| `shouldExpandNode`     | `(level, value, field?) => boolean`                       | `allExpanded`   | Initial-expand strategy per node                                                   |
| `clickToExpandNode`    | `boolean`                                                 | `false`         | Clicking the field label also toggles the node                                     |
| `beforeExpandChange`   | `(event: NodeExpandingEvent) => boolean`                  | —               | Return `false` to veto an expand/collapse transition                               |
| `compactTopLevel`      | `boolean`                                                 | `false`         | Spread root-object entries rather than nesting under a single root expander        |
| `string`, `number`, …  | `Snippet<[{ value, field?, level }]>`                     | —               | Optional per-type renderer overrides                                               |

Any additional HTML attributes (`aria-*`, `data-*`, `id`, `class`, etc.) are forwarded onto the root `<div role="tree">`.

## Basic Usage

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
    import { JsonView, defaultStyles } from '@humanspeak/svelte-json-view-lite'

    const data = {
        name: 'Ada Lovelace',
        tags: ['admin', 'beta'],
        active: true,
        joined: new Date('2024-01-15')
    }
</script>

<JsonView {data} style={defaultStyles} />
```

## Theme Presets

Two themes ship out of the box:

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
    import { JsonView, defaultStyles, darkStyles } from '@humanspeak/svelte-json-view-lite'
</script>

<JsonView data={json} style={darkStyles} />
```

Override individual slots by spreading:

```svelte
<JsonView
    {data}
    style={{
        ...defaultStyles,
        stringValue: 'my-custom-string-class'
    }}
/>
```

## CSS Variable Theming

Every color is exposed as a CSS custom property on the root container. Override at any ancestor selector without swapping the `style` prop:

| Variable            | Default (light)    | Default (dark)       | Applies to                                 |
| ------------------- | ------------------ | -------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| `--sjv-background`  | `#eee`             | `rgb(0, 43, 54)`     | Root container background                  |
| `--sjv-label`       | `#000000`          | `rgb(253, 246, 227)` | Field label text                           |
| `--sjv-punctuation` | `#000000`          | `rgb(253, 246, 227)` | Brackets, colons, commas                   |
| `--sjv-string`      | `rgb(42, 63, 60)`  | `rgb(203, 75, 22)`   | String values                              |
| `--sjv-number`      | `#0b75f5`          | `rgb(211, 54, 130)`  | Number & BigInt values                     |
| `--sjv-boolean`     | `rgb(70, 144, 56)` | `rgb(174, 129, 255)` | Boolean values                             |
| `--sjv-null`        | `#df113a`          | `rgb(129, 181, 172)` | `null` literal                             |
| `--sjv-undefined`   | `#df113a`          | `rgb(129, 181, 172)` | `undefined` literal                        |
| `--sjv-other`       | `#43413d`          | `rgb(38, 139, 210)`  | Dates, functions, symbols, etc.            |
| `--sjv-expander`    | `#000000`          | `rgb(253, 246, 227)` | `▸` / `▾` icons and collapsed `...` marker |

## Snippet Overrides

Every primitive type plus the field label can be replaced with a typed Svelte snippet:

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
    import {
        JsonView,
        type DateSnippetProps,
        type StringSnippetProps
    } from '@humanspeak/svelte-json-view-lite'
</script>

<JsonView {data}>
    {#snippet date({ value }: DateSnippetProps)}
        <span title={value.toISOString()}>{relative(value)}</span>
    {/snippet}
    {#snippet string({ value }: StringSnippetProps)}
        {#if /^https?:/.test(value)}
            <a href={value}>{value}</a>
        {:else}
            "{value}"
        {/if}
    {/snippet}
</JsonView>
```

Available snippet names: `string`, `number`, `boolean`, `null`, `undefined`, `bigint`, `date`, `function`, `label`. Their typed prop interfaces (`StringSnippetProps`, `LabelSnippetProps`, etc.) are all exported from the package root.

## Expand Strategies

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
    import {
        JsonView,
        collapseAllNested,
        allExpanded
    } from '@humanspeak/svelte-json-view-lite'
</script>

<JsonView {data} shouldExpandNode={collapseAllNested} />
<JsonView {data} shouldExpandNode={allExpanded} />
<JsonView {data} shouldExpandNode={(level) => level < 2} />
```

## Migration from react-json-view-lite

| React                       | Svelte                                                       |
| --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `style={defaultStyles}`     | `style={defaultStyles}`                                      |
| `clickToExpandNode`         | `clickToExpandNode`                                          |
| `compactTopLevel`           | `compactTopLevel`                                            |
| `shouldExpandNode`          | `shouldExpandNode`                                           |
| `beforeExpandChange`        | `beforeExpandChange`                                         |
| `style.ariaLables` (typoed) | `style.ariaLabels` (typo accepted at runtime with a warning) |
| — (not supported)           | Per-type `Snippet` overrides                                 |

Everything else preserves the React API exactly.

## Accessibility

- Root element has `role="tree"` with `aria-label="JSON view"` (overridable)
- Every expandable node is `role="treeitem"` with live `aria-expanded` and, when open, `aria-controls` pointing at its child `<ul role="group">`
- Keyboard support follows the WAI-ARIA 1.2 Treeview pattern (`ArrowRight`/`ArrowLeft` expand/collapse, `ArrowDown`/`ArrowUp` move focus with wrap-around, roving `tabindex` keeps only one expander in the tab order)

## Public Exports

Component:

- `JsonView` (default and named export)

Theme presets:

- `defaultStyles` (light)
- `darkStyles`
- `defaultAriaLabels`

Expand strategies:

- `allExpanded`
- `collapseAllNested`

Types:

- `Props`, `StyleProps`, `AriaLabels`, `NodeExpandingEvent`, `SnippetOverrides`
- Per-type snippet props: `StringSnippetProps`, `NumberSnippetProps`, `BooleanSnippetProps`, `NullSnippetProps`, `UndefinedSnippetProps`, `BigIntSnippetProps`, `DateSnippetProps`, `FunctionSnippetProps`, `LabelSnippetProps`

- Documentation: https://jsonview.svelte.page
- Repository: https://github.com/humanspeak/svelte-json-view-lite
- NPM: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@humanspeak/svelte-json-view-lite
